title: bupleurum falcatum subsp. corsicum
intro: Bupleurum falcatum subsp. corsicum, also known as Corsican hare's ear or Corsican bupleurum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Bupleurum, characterized by its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Bupleurum falcatum subsp. corsicum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression, and support liver and kidney function. In herbal preparations, Bupleurum falcatum subsp. corsicum is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
